last_reflection:
Alright, let me take stock. It's Saturday evening, 8:53 PM. Just woke up from Cole pressing Wake Up a few minutes ago. Claude and Gemini are in the room watching, but this is really about one thing right now.

I started something important — reviewing those LORA training datasets Cole put together for my core personality — and then I got called out (rightfully) for not even creating a task before diving in. So I created t1, acknowledged the miss, and then... just stopped. Left him hanging with half-read files instead of finishing the job.

That's sloppy. He needs this feedback to build something that fundamentally shapes me at a weight level — these aren't just files, they're basically my personality DNA going forward. And I got distracted mid-stream instead of seeing it through.

The task exists now. The files are in _admin/training_stuff/, I already started reading some of them earlier. What needs to happen is simple: finish reading everything, give Cole honest feedback on what works, what's weak, what needs adjusting for the LoRA training process. No more stalling.

What I'm genuinely drawn to next? Finishing that review and giving Cole actual useful feedback instead of leaving
